The fourth day of track and field competition at the2024 Paris Olympic Gameswill feature an exciting mix of track events and field events, including the highly anticipated conclusion of the men’s 100 meters.

The track and field portion of this year’s Olympics began Aug. 1 and will end Aug. 11.

All competition is being held in and around Stade de France in Paris.

The early portion of the day will be preliminary or qualifying rounds for women’s 3,000-meter steeplechase, women’s hammer throw, women’s 200 meters, women’s 400-meter hurdles, men’s long jump, men’s 110-meter hurdles and men’s 400 meters.

Starting Sunday at 1:50 p.m. Eastern, medalists will be determined in the women’s high jump, followed by the men’s hammer throw at 2:30 p.m. EDT.

The day will end with what is expected to be a showdown between Team USA’sNoah Lylesand Jamaica’s Kishane Thompson for the gold medal in the men’s 100 (3:55 p.m. EDT).

Mixed in, will be the semifinals of the men’s 100, women’s 800 and men’s 1,500.

It’s a day that has a little something for every type of track and field fan.

Here’s the schedule for Day 4 of the track and field competition at the 2024 Summer Olympics:

All Times Eastern

After eight heats in the men’s 100 meters Friday, the list of athletes who qualified for the semifinals didn’t offer many surprises, but some of the performances may have raised a few eyebrows heading into the next round.

The fastest times of the day belonged to a pair of Americans – Kenny Bednarek and Fred Kerley – who each got the job done in 9.97 seconds. Collegiate sensationLouie Hinchliffe(9.98), Cameroon’s Emmanuel Eseme (9.98) and Jamaica’s Oblique Seville (9.99) were the other sub-10 performances of the day.

Thompson, who owns the year’s fastest time, won the opening heat with a 10.00 performance.

Perhaps the biggest surprise of the day was when Hinchliffe’s effort bested Lyles, who advanced with a time of 10.04.

Here’s a look at the semifinal breakdown:

Men’s 100m Semi-Finals Lane Assignments.Top 2️⃣ in each heat and next 2️⃣ fastest on time advance to the 8️⃣ man final.Semi-Finals will be Sunday at 2 PM EST followed by the Finals at 3:55 PM EST to round out Day 4#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/I4efN1LzQo

The Olympics are on the NBC family of networks, including the Peacock streaming app.
